method: Web-research fan-out; schemas verified by WebFetch of actual GitHub README/markdown/proto files. row_count_if_known null unless stated in source. Column names quoted VERBATIM where confidence=high.
headline: None of these is an LLM-serving cold-start / model-load / KV-cache telemetry trace. The only true LLM-serving releases (Azure LLM/LMM 2023/24/25) are arrival-timestamp + token-count ONLY. The richest MEASURED cold-start signal in any public release is Huawei 2025 (sir-lab/data-release) but it is FaaS cold start (container/pod + code/dependency deploy), NOT LLM model-weight loading. Best migration/queue proxies are Google 2019 (QUEUE+EVICT+FAIL/KILL) and Alibaba GPU v2020 (queue delay + GPU util).

artifacts.artifact_name: Huawei Cloud Public Cold Start Traces 2025
artifacts.paper: Serverless Cold Starts and Where to Find Them (EuroSys'25; Huawei SIR Lab / Edinburgh)
signal_category: model_load (FaaS code/dep-load proxy, NOT LLM weights)
artifacts.contains_queue_metrics: partial (no queue-depth field; scheduling cost present)
artifacts.contains_autoscaling_metrics: partial (per-cold-start requestID = request that triggered scaling decision; num_pods over time)
artifacts.notes: 31 days, 5 regions. (1) Cold-start events (per-request): day,time,clusterName,funcName,userID,requestID,totalCost_cold_start,podAllocationCost,deployCodeCost,deployDependencyCost,schedulingCost,podID (podID encodes warm-pool class e.g. pool24-600-512). (2) Request events table (day 30): per-request worker/frontend cost breakdown, cpu_usage, memory_usage, requestBodySize. (3) Function metadata. (4) Per-minute time series, 19 metrics each with avg/std + quantiles {0,.25,.5,.75,.9,.95,.99,1.0}: num_cold_starts, num_pods, podAllocationCost, deployCodeCost, deployDependencyCost, schedulingCost, totalCost_cold_start, ... CRITICAL LIMIT: FaaS, not LLM-serving; deployCodeCost/deployDependencyCost is code/dependency load not LLM weight load; no migration, no cache-hit, no queue-depth, no GPU.

artifacts.paper: How Does It Function? Characterizing Long-term Trends in Production Serverless Workloads (SoCC'23; arXiv 2312.10127)
artifacts.notes: 235 days span / 141 days private (200 funcs) + 26 days public (5093 funcs). AGGREGATED per-minute/second means only, 8 metrics: Function ID, Timestamp, Requests, Function delay, Platform delay (scheduling+network proxy), CPU %, Memory %, Instances. NO cold-start column (2025 release is the cold-start one), NO p95/p99 (means), NO per-request, NO GPU/cache/migration.

artifacts.artifact_name: FaaSNet trace (Alibaba Function Compute cold-start logs)
artifacts.paper: FaaSNet: Scalable and Fast Provisioning of Custom Serverless Container Runtimes at Alibaba Cloud Function Compute (USENIX ATC'21)
artifacts.public_downloadable: conditional — gated behind a Google Form survey, not a direct in-repo file
artifacts.notes: 24h production logs May 2021, two datacenters. Per-request fields: __time__, functionName, latency (cold-start latency s), runtime, memoryMB. TRUE per-request cold-start but counts only SYSTEM-level latency (container init), NOT end-to-end. FaaS not LLM. Download gated (could not retrieve file).

artifacts.paper: Borg: the Next Generation (Tirmazi et al., EuroSys'20); trace format v3
signal_category: tail_latency (tail_cpu_usage_distribution — CPU util tail, NOT request latency)
artifacts.notes: 8 Borg cells, May 2019. InstanceEvent EventType enum (verified from clusterdata_trace_format_v3.proto): SUBMIT, QUEUE, ENABLE, SCHEDULE, EVICT, FAIL, FINISH, KILL, LOST, UPDATE_PENDING, UPDATE_RUNNING. instance_usage has cpu_usage_distribution (10-bucket) + tail_cpu_usage_distribution (p91-p99). NO GPU, cold-start, model-load, cache, or autoscaling-decision events. Job-level / resource-level, NOT LLM-serving.

artifacts.paper: Google cluster-usage traces: format + schema (Reiss & Wilkes, 2011)
artifacts.notes: 29 days, ~12.5k machines, May 2011. task_events EventType: 0=SUBMIT,1=SCHEDULE,2=EVICT,3=FAIL,4=FINISH,5=KILL,6=LOST,7=UPDATE_PENDING,8=UPDATE_RUNNING. task_usage 5-min CPU/mem time-series. NO GPU/cold-start/model-load/cache. Same proxy story as 2019 without the explicit QUEUE event.

artifacts.artifact_name: Alibaba cluster-trace-gpu-v2020 (PAI / MLaaS in the Wild)
artifacts.paper: MLaaS in the Wild: Workload Analysis and Scheduling in Large-Scale Heterogeneous GPU Clusters (NSDI'22)
artifacts.contains_queue_metrics: proxy (derived queueing delay; paper notes 'long queueing delays')
artifacts.notes: Jul-Aug 2020. pai_sensor_table has gpu_wrk_util, avg/max_gpu_wrk_mem, plan_gpu fractional. status Running/Terminated/Failed/Waiting. GPU TRAINING/ML, NOT LLM-serving: no cold-start, model-load, KV-cache, autoscaling events, or per-request inference latency. Already known to the repo lineage (Alibaba GPU v2023 is ingested).

artifacts.paper: Beware of Fragmentation: Scheduling GPU-Sharing Workloads with Fragmentation Gradient Descent (USENIX ATC'23)
artifacts.notes: NOT a runtime util trace — a K8s-scheduler-simulator dataset (openb_*). Scheduling SNAPSHOTS (creation/scheduled/deletion timestamps), pod_phase Succeeded/Running/Pending/Failed. No runtime GPU time-series, no queue-wait, no cold-start/cache. Thinner than v2020. (This is the GPU trace already in-repo lineage.)

artifacts.paper: Characterizing Microservice Dependency and Performance: Alibaba Trace Analysis (SoCC'21)
artifacts.notes: 12h, ~20k microservices. MSCallGraph(timestamp,traceid,rpcid,um,rpctype,interface,dm,rt). rt = per-call response time (ms) = genuine per-request LATENCY; call-graph (um/dm) = routing-instability proxy. NO failure/timeout label, NO queue-depth, NO cold-start/cache/GPU. p95/p99 derivable from rt. NOT LLM-serving but closest public per-request LATENCY trace.

artifacts.paper: The Power of Prediction: Microservice Auto Scaling via Workload Learning (SoCC'22)
signal_category: autoscaling-RELATED (paper is autoscaling; trace has NO scale-event labels)
artifacts.notes: 13 days. Adds service ID in call graph + instance-level RT/MCR. MSRTMCR per-instance RT/MCR for consumer/provider RPC, DB, MQ, HTTP. Paper is about AUTO-SCALING but the released trace has NO explicit scale-up/down EVENT labels — only workload+RT to feed a scaler. NO queue/timeout/failure/cold-start/GPU/cache.

artifacts.paper: Splitwise: Efficient Generative LLM Inference Using Phase Splitting (ISCA'24)
artifacts.notes: Columns verbatim: TIMESTAMP, ContextTokens, GeneratedTokens. Confirmed = the Splitwise dataset. Only arrival + token counts. No latency/TTFT/failure/model-id/cold-start/KV-cache.

artifacts.paper: DynamoLLM: Designing LLM Inference Clusters for Performance and Energy Efficiency (HPCA'25)
artifacts.notes: Columns verbatim (3): TIMESTAMP, ContextTokens, GeneratedTokens. May 10-19 2024 (~10 days). Same shape as 2023; already partially used in-repo (data/external/azure_llm_2024). Latency meant to be benchmarked externally — not in trace.

artifacts.paper: ModServe: Modality- and Stage-Aware Resource Disaggregation for Scalable Multimodal Model Serving (SoCC'25)
artifacts.notes: Columns verbatim (4): TIMESTAMP, NumImages, ContextTokens, GeneratedTokens. Oct 2024. Multimodal variant; still no latency/failure/cold-start/cache.

artifacts.paper: Serverless in the Wild (USENIX ATC'20, Shahrad et al.)
signal_category: autoscaling/cold-start PAPER but released DATA has NO cold-start field; per-minute invocation counts
file_types: CSV (3 families x up to 14 daily files)
artifacts.notes: 14 days July 2019. invocations_per_function (per-minute counts), function_durations_percentiles (Average/Count/Min/Max/percentiles), app_memory_percentiles. CRITICAL: duration 'does NOT include cold start time' (stated explicitly). The canonical cold-start PAPER, but released data has NO cold-start column — only counts + warm-execution percentiles. Not per-request.

artifacts.paper: Faster and Cheaper Serverless Computing on Harvested Resources (SOSP'21)
artifacts.notes: Columns verbatim (4): app, func, end_timestamp(s), duration(s). 2 weeks Jan 2021. The ONE Azure trace with TRUE per-request duration + app/func identity. But NO cold-start label, NO failure/timeout label. Execution duration not request latency.

artifacts.paper: Faa$T: A Transparent Auto-Scaling Cache for Serverless Applications (SoCC'21)
signal_category: raw I/O for cache_hit (derivable via simulation, NOT labeled)
artifacts.notes: Columns: Timestamp, AnonRegion, AnonUserId, AnonAppName, AnonFunctionInvocationId, AnonBlobName, BlobType, AnonBlobETag, BlobBytes, Read, Write. Per-blob-access events. Faa$T paper is about a CACHE, but released trace has NO cache-hit/miss label — raw blob R/W you could simulate a cache over. No latency.
